title: agrostis alba subvar. pumila
intro: Agrostis alba subvar. pumila, also known as dwarf bent grass, is a low-growing, perennial grass native to the Arctic tundra and alpine regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Agrostis alba subvar. pumila include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve overall well-being. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used in salves and creams to aid in wound healing and skin care.
